title: Preliminary Estimates of RSV Burden for 2024-2025 | RSV | CDC
text:

The in-season cumulative estimates of RSV burden are preliminary and change week-by-week as updated RSV hospitalization data are reported to CDC via RSV-NET. Updated reports include both new admissions that have occurred during the reporting week and also patients admitted in previous weeks that may not have been included in earlier reporting to CDC.
It is important to note that these preliminary disease burden estimates reflect the overall burden that RSV has on the U.S. population. While young children and adults who are older or have certain risk factors are at increased risk for severe RSV, they are affected very differently. RSV is the leading cause of infant hospitalization, but many fewer infants die from RSV compared to adults who are older or have certain risk factors.
